children.heading: Average yield and acreage average for historic peanut producers
children.label: a Average yield and acreage average for historic peanut producers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine, for each historic peanut producer, the average yield for peanuts on each farm on which the historic peanut producer planted peanuts for harvest for the 1998 through 2001 crop years, excluding any crop year in which the producer did not plant or was prevented from planting peanuts.

children.heading: Assigned yields
children.label: B Assigned yields
children.text: For the purposes of determining the 4-year average yield for an historic peanut producer under this paragraph, the historic peanut producer may elect to substitute for a farm, for not more than 3 of the 1998 through 2001 crop years in which the producer planted peanuts on the farm, the average yield for peanuts produced in the county in which the farm is located for the 1990 through 1997 crop years.

children.label: i
children.text: Acreage planted to peanuts on each farm on which the historic peanut producer planted peanuts for harvest for the 1998 through 2001 crop years.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any acreage on each farm that the historic peanut producer was prevented from planting to peanuts during the 1998 through 2001 crop years because of drought, flood, or other natural disaster, or other condition beyond the control of the historic peanut producer, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Inclusion of all 4 years in average
children.label: B Inclusion of all 4 years in average
children.text: For the purposes of determining the 4-year acreage average for an historic peanut producer under this paragraph, the Secretary shall not exclude any crop year in which the producer did not plant peanuts.

children.heading: Proportional shares
children.label: C Proportional shares
children.text: If more than 1 historic peanut producer shared in the risk of producing the crop on a farm, the historic peanut producers shall receive their proportional share of the number of acres planted (or prevented from being planted) to peanuts for harvest on the farm based on the sharing arrangement that was in effect among the producers for the crop.

children.heading: Time for determinations
children.label: 3 Time for determinations
children.text: The Secretary shall make the determinations required by this subsection as soon as practicable after .May 13, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: when an historic peanut producer is no longer living;

children.label: B
children.text: when an entity composed of historic peanut producers has been dissolved; or

children.label: C
children.text: in other appropriate situations, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Assignment of average yields and average acreage to farms
children.label: b Assignment of average yields and average acreage to farms

children.heading: Assignment by historic peanut producers
children.label: 1 Assignment by historic peanut producers
children.text: The Secretary shall give each historic peanut producer an opportunity to assign the average peanut yield and average acreage determined under subsection (a) for each farm of the historic peanut producer to cropland on that farm or another farm in the same State or a contiguous State.

children.label: A
children.text: the historic peanut producer making the assignment produced peanuts in that State during at least 1 of the 1998 through 2001 crop years; or

children.label: B
children.text: as of , the historic peanut producer is a producer on a farm in that State.March 31, 2003

children.label: A
children.text: Notice that the opportunity to make the assignments is being provided only once.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the limitation in paragraph (2) on their ability to make the assignments.

children.label: C
children.text: Information regarding the manner in which the assignments must be made and the time periods and manner in which notice of the assignments must be submitted to the Secretary.

children.heading: Assignment deadlines
children.label: 4 Assignment deadlines
children.text: Not later than , an historic peanut producer shall submit to the Secretary notice of the assignments made by the producer under this subsection. If an historic peanut producer fails to submit the notice by that date, the notice shall be submitted in such other manner as the Secretary may prescribe.March 31, 2003

children.heading: Payment yield
children.label: c Payment yield
children.text: The average of all of the yields assigned by historic peanut producers under subsection (b) to a farm shall be considered to be the payment yield for that farm for the purpose of making direct payments and counter-cyclical payments under this subchapter.

children.heading: Base acres for peanuts
children.label: d Base acres for peanuts
children.text: Subject to subsection (e), the total number of acres assigned by historic peanut producers under subsection (b) to a farm shall be considered to be the farm’s base acres for peanuts for the purpose of making direct payments and counter-cyclical payments under this subchapter.

children.heading: Treatment of conservation reserve contract acreage
children.label: e Treatment of conservation reserve contract acreage
A
children.text: A conservation reserve contract entered into under section 1231 of the Food Security Act of 1985 () with respect to the farm expires or is voluntarily terminated.16 U.S.C. 3831

children.label: B
children.text: Cropland is released from coverage under a conservation reserve contract by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special payment rules
children.label: 2 Special payment rules
children.text: For the crop year in which a base acres for peanuts adjustment under paragraph (1) is first made, the owner of the farm shall elect to receive either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments with respect to the acreage added to the farm under this subsection or a prorated payment under the conservation reserve contract, but not both.

children.heading: Prevention of excess base acres for peanuts
children.label: f Prevention of excess base acres for peanuts

children.heading: Required reduction
children.label: 1 Required reduction
children.text: If the sum of the base acres for peanuts for a farm, together with the acreage described in paragraph (2), exceeds the actual cropland acreage of the farm, the Secretary shall reduce the base acres for peanuts for the farm or the base acres for 1 or more covered commodities under subchapter I for the farm so that the sum of the base acres for peanuts and acreage described in paragraph (2) does not exceed the actual cropland acreage of the farm.

children.label: A
children.text: Any base acres for the farm under subchapter I.

children.label: B
children.text: Any acreage on the farm enrolled in the conservation reserve program or wetlands reserve program under chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 3830

children.label: C
children.text: Any other acreage on the farm enrolled in a conservation program for which payments are made in exchange for not producing an agricultural commodity on the acreage.

children.heading: Selection of acres
children.label: 3 Selection of acres
children.text: The Secretary shall give the owner of the farm the opportunity to select the base acres for peanuts or the subchapter I base acres against which the reduction required by paragraph (1) will be made.

children.heading: Exception for double-cropped acreage
children.label: 4 Exception for double-cropped acreage
children.text: In applying paragraph (1), the Secretary shall make an exception in the case of double cropping, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Coordinated application of requirements
children.label: 5 Coordinated application of requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall take into account when applying the requirements of this subsection.section 7911(g) of this title

children.heading: Permanent reduction in base acres for peanuts
children.label: g Permanent reduction in base acres for peanuts
children.text: The owner of a farm may reduce, at any time, the base acres for peanuts assigned to the farm. The reduction shall be permanent and made in the manner prescribed by the Secretary.
